steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage presents two key facts: the ridge surface is “fairly dry” with “few other plants and animals,” and N. pudica uniquely has underground pitchers. This pairing strongly suggests the plant has adapted to where the actual food source is — below the sparse surface.

steps.title: Apply the signal to find the explanation
steps.content:
Choice D captures the reasoning: N. pudica evolved underground traps to access more prey than would surface traps.

The other choices don't follow from the evidence —
  • A addresses undiscovered species rather than this plant's adaptation
  • B misidentifies pitcher function as moisture absorption
  • C misreads the mechanism as passive burial rather than evolved design
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
